title: Coelachne perpusilla
taxonomy: Coelachne perpusilla is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Andropogoneae and the genus Coelachne. The genus Coelachne is a group of tropical grasses found primarily in the Australian region. Coelachne perpusilla is native to northern Australia and New Guinea. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary history and its relationships with other organisms.

intro: Coelachne perpusilla, also known as the Australian jointgrass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Australian Aboriginal culture, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelachne perpusilla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Coelachne perpusilla is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
